focusPoints.label: Quality bar in concrete, usable terms
focusPoints.reason: What “good” looks like in his brand design work right now, with examples, so feedback doesn’t stay abstract.

focusPoints.label: Whether feedback turned into behavior changes
focusPoints.reason: Whether the prior feedback he’s received translated into specific changes in his outputs, or stayed at the level of general advice.

focusPoints.label: How he defines “good work” at his level
focusPoints.reason: What he believes the expectations and success bar are for a junior brand designer, so you can calibrate and reduce subjectivity.

focusPoints.label: Near-term growth focus and what blocks it
focusPoints.reason: What he feels blocked on in his development, and which one practice for the next few weeks would move him fastest.

name: Carlos
notes: For Carlos, this performance conversation needs to make the quality bar concrete and usable. I want to understand whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract. I also need to hear how he defines good work right now and where he feels blocked in growth. Without that shared baseline, feedback can feel subjective and demotivating. My goal is to align on specific expectations, examples, and one near-term development focus he can practice immediately.
